perturbation.original_text: Section I II: Business Confidential Information (CI) For purposes of this agreement, C I shall mean any confidential , proprietary or trade secret information that is owned or controlled, or is specifically marked or identified as “Confidential ”, by VELCO prior to disclosure to Recipient . It includes information of third parties in VELCO ’s possession that VELCO is obligated to keep confidential . CI subject to this Agreement may be in intangible form, such as information communicated orally or by visual observation, or may be embodied in tangible form, such as a document. The term "document" includes written memoranda, drawings, sketches, maps, training materials, specifications, notebook entries, photographs, graphic representations, firmware, computer information or software, informati on communicated by other electronic or magnetic media, or models.
perturbation.changed_text: Section I II: Business Confidential Information (CI) For purposes of this agreement, C I shall mean any confidential , proprietary or trade secret information that is owned or controlled, or is specifically marked or identified as “Confidential ”, by VELCO prior to disclosure to Recipient . It includes information of third parties in VELCO ’s possession that VELCO is obligated to keep confidential .
perturbation.explanation: By removing "CI subject to this Agreement may be in intangible form, such as information communicated orally or by visual observation, or may be embodied in tangible form, such as a document. The term "document" includes written memoranda, drawings, sketches, maps, training materials, specifications, notebook entries, photographs, graphic representations, firmware, computer information or software, informati on communicated by other electronic or magnetic media, or models.", it creates an in-text contradiction with Section IV, paragraph 6, because the latter references Classified Information including "electronic or paper media" implying that electronic information can be part of it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. In the event that VELCO, in its sole discre tion, so requests , the Recipient will promptly return to VELCO or destroy in accordance with VELCO’s instructions , all Classified Information, including all copies, reproductions, compilations, analyses or extracts thereof, whether electronic or paper media.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined CI as including intangible forms like oral communication and tangible forms like documents, explicitly including electronic media within the definition of 'document.' The changed text omits this specific definition. The contradicted text (Section IV, paragraph 6) refers to 'Classified Information, including all copies, reproductions... whether electronic or paper media.' By removing the explicit inclusion of electronic media in the definition of CI, the changed text creates a contradiction. Section IV now implies that electronic media can be Classified Information, but Section I II, as modified, no longer explicitly includes electronic information as part of CI. This creates an inconsistency because the definition of CI no longer aligns with how the term is used elsewhere in the contract, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. The Recipient shall exercise reasonable care to maintain the confidentiality and secrecy of the Classified Information , and shall not divulge C lassified Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the VELCO . The foregoing notwithstanding, the Recipient may disclose classified information to its employees or contractors (hereafter Representatives ) to the extent each such R epresentative has a need to know such information to conduct the work referenced in the Whereas clauses, and shall comply with Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement. The Recipient is responsible for its Representative’s breach of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. The Recipient shall exercise reasonable care to maintain the confidentiality and secrecy of the Classified Information , and shall not divulge C lassified Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the VELCO .
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "The foregoing notwithstanding, the Recipient may disclose classified information to its employees or contractors (hereafter Representatives ) to the extent each such R epresentative has a need to know such information to conduct the work referenced in the Whereas clauses, and shall comply with Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement. The Recipient is responsible for its Representative’s breach of the terms of this Agreement.", the contract now contradicts itself. It says the Recipient cannot disclose classified information to any third party, but Section IV, paragraph 4, says "A Recipient will not use or allow a Representative to use Classified Information directly or indirectly for any illegal purpose, non-legitimate purpose , or any purpose other than the work referenced in the Whereas clause". This section mentions "Representative" when the text doesn't state what is it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Recipient and each of its Representatives shall use all C lassified Information disclosed by VELCO solely in connection with the work referenced in the Whereas clau ses and shall not use, directly or indirectly, any information for any other purpose without VELCO’s prior written consent. A Recipient will not use or allow a Representative to use Classified Information directly or indirectly for any illegal purpose, non-legitimate purpose , or any purpose other than the work referenced in the Whereas clause .
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly allowed the Recipient to disclose classified information to its Representatives under certain conditions, while holding the Recipient responsible for any breaches by said Representatives. The changed text removes this allowance, stating that the Recipient cannot divulge Classified Information to any third party. This directly contradicts Section IV, paragraph 4 (the 'Contradicted Text'), which discusses the use of Classified Information by 'Representatives.' The contradiction lies in the fact that the contract now prohibits disclosure to any third party, while another section anticipates and regulates the actions of Representatives. This creates an inconsistency, as the contract refers to Representatives using classified information, but now states that sharing the information with them is prohibited.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Recipient and each of its Representatives shall use all C lassified Information disclosed by VELCO solely in connection with the work referenced in the Whereas clau ses and shall not use, directly or indirectly, any information for any other purpose without VELCO’s prior written consent. A Recipient will not use or allow a Representative to use Classified Information directly or indirectly for any illegal purpose, non-legitimate purpose , or any purpose other than the work referenced in the Whereas clause . 5. In the event that the Recipient is required to disclose C lassified Information by subpoena, law or other directive of a court, administrative agency , or arbitration panel, the Recipient will provide VELCO with immediate notice of such request in order to enable VELCO (and Recipient shall cooperate fully with VELCO) to se ek an appropriate protective order or other remedy . Recipient shall also consult with VELCO to identify steps available t o quash or narrow the scope of such request or legal process . VELCO may in its sole discretion waive compliance, in whole or in part, with the terms of this Agreement. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or VELCO waives compliance, the Recipient shall furnish only that portion of the C lassified Information which the Recipient/ ’s counsel advises is legally required to comply with a governmental order or directive and shall exercise best efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such C lassified Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Recipient and each of its Representatives shall use all C lassified Information disclosed by VELCO solely in connection with the work referenced in the Whereas clau ses and shall not use, directly or indirectly, any information for any other purpose without VELCO’s prior written consent. A Recipient will not use or allow a Representative to use Classified Information directly or indirectly for any illegal purpose, non-legitimate purpose , or any purpose other than the work referenced in the Whereas clause .
perturbation.explanation: Removing Section IV, paragraph 5 "In the event that the Recipient is required to disclose C lassified Information by subpoena, law or other directive of a court, administrative agency , or arbitration panel, the Recipient will provide VELCO with immediate notice of such request in order to enable VELCO (and Recipient shall cooperate fully with VELCO) to se ek an appropriate protective order or other remedy . Recipient shall also consult with VELCO to identify steps available t o quash or narrow the scope of such request or legal process . VELCO may in its sole discretion waive compliance, in whole or in part, with the terms of this Agreement. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or VELCO waives compliance, the Recipient shall furnish only that portion of the C lassified Information which the Recipient/ ’s counsel advises is legally required to comply with a governmental order or directive and shall exercise best efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such C lassified Information." will contradict Section IV, paragraph 3. If Recipient is required to disclose Classified Information, Section IV, paragraph 3 states he/she needs permission of VELCO.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. The Recipient shall exercise reasonable care to maintain the confidentiality and secrecy of the Classified Information , and shall not divulge C lassified Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the VELCO . The foregoing notwithstanding, the Recipient may disclose classified information to its employees or contractors (hereafter Representatives ) to the extent each such R epresentative has a need to know such information to conduct the work referenced in the Whereas clauses, and shall comply with Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement. The Recipient is responsible for its Representative’s breach of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a clause (paragraph 5) detailing the recipient's obligations in the event they are legally compelled to disclose Classified Information, including notifying VELCO and cooperating to seek a protective order. The changed text omits this clause. This creates a contradiction with paragraph 3 of Section IV, which states that the Recipient shall not divulge Classified Information to any third party without the prior written consent of VELCO. The original text allowed for an exception to this rule in the event of a subpoena, law, or court order. By removing paragraph 5, the changed text now implies that there is no exception to the rule in paragraph 3, creating a direct conflict with the implied previous allowance and necessitating explicit written consent in all cases, even those where legally obligated to disclose.

perturbation.original_text: Section I: BES Cyber System Information (BCSI) For purposes of this Agreement, “ BCSI ” shall mean: (i) all information designated as such by VELCO, whether furnished before or after the date hereof, whether oral, written or recorded/electronic, and regardless of the manner in which it is furnished; and (ii) all reports, summaries, compilations, analyses, notes or other information which contain such information. Written information containing BCSI furnished by VELCO shall be labeled “ Confide ntial BCSI .”
perturbation.changed_text: Section I: BES Cyber System Information (BCSI) For purposes of this Agreement, “ BCSI ” shall mean: (i) all information designated as such by VELCO, whether furnished before or after the date hereof, whether oral, written or recorded/electronic, and regardless of the manner in which it is furnished; and (ii) all reports, summaries, compilations, analyses, notes or other information which contain such information.
perturbation.explanation: Removing "Written information containing BCSI furnished by VELCO shall be labeled “ Confide ntial BCSI ."" creates a contradiction with Section IV, paragraph 1. Because it makes reference to "Information labeled “ Confidential BCSI ”, that doesn't exist.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Information labeled “ Confidential BCSI ” shall be treated and protected in accordance with the enforceable version of the NERC CIP -011 Cyber Security – Information Protection standard and shall require a Recipient background check and Recipient’s successful completion of VELCO -designated training in addition to compliance with the other requirements of and protections in this Agreement ; and 2. Recipient shall keep all Classified Information in a secure place.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that 'Written information containing BCSI furnished by VELCO shall be labeled “Confide ntial BCSI.”' The changed text removes this requirement. The contradicted text refers to 'Information labeled “Confidential BCSI”' and outlines how it should be handled. By removing the requirement that written BCSI be labeled as 'Confidential BCSI,' the changed text creates a contradiction because the contradicted text relies on the existence of such labeled information. Without the labeling, the instructions in the contradicted text become unclear or impossible to implement, creating a direct conflict.
